title: Free Men Unite Against Slavery
story: Brave free African Americans gathered to fight injustice. In 1830, on September 20, the First Negro Convention of Free Men met. They made a bold decision to stop buying products made by enslaved people. This peaceful protest aimed to weaken the cruel system of slavery by hitting slaveholders in their wallets.
activity_question: What did the Free Men agree to do at the convention?
activity_choices: Write a letter to the President
follow_up_question: Why do you think boycotting slave-produced goods might help fight against slavery?

title: Trains Roll into Dutch History
story: Choo-choo! The Netherlands welcomed its first railroad in 1839. On September 20, steam engines puffed to life, connecting the cities of Amsterdam and Haarlem. This new iron road changed how people traveled, making trips faster and easier. It marked the beginning of a transportation revolution in the country.
activity_question: What two cities did the first railroad in the Netherlands connect?
follow_up_question: How do you think travel changed for people after the railroad opened?
